title: Asset
description: A non-property asset: financial accounts, personal property, vehicles, digital assets, business interests, Islamic finance instruments, etc.

id.description: Unique identifier for this asset within the INHERIT document.

name.description: Display name for this asset. Should be specific enough to identify the item during probate.

category.$comment: bank_account: current or checking account. savings_account: deposit or savings account. investment: stocks, bonds, funds, ISAs. pension: occupational or personal pension. shares: individual company shares. premium_bonds: UK NS&I Premium Bonds. cryptocurrency: Bitcoin, Ethereum, etc. insurance: life insurance or endowment. vehicle: car, motorcycle, boat, etc. jewellery: rings, necklaces, watches. art: paintings, sculptures, prints. antiques: items of historical value. collectibles: stamps, coins, memorabilia. furniture: household furniture. electronics: computers, phones, cameras. musical_instruments: pianos, guitars, etc. books: valuable or collectible books. clothing: valuable clothing items. kitchenware: valuable kitchen items. sports_equipment: golf clubs, etc. firearms: shotguns, rifles (require licensing). wine_and_spirits: wine collections, rare spirits. tools: workshop tools and equipment. garden_and_outdoor: garden machinery, outdoor equipment. business_interest: share in a business or partnership. intellectual_property: patents, copyrights, trademarks. domain_name: internet domain names. social_media_account: accounts with commercial value. digital_subscription: transferable digital subscriptions. sukuk: Islamic bond. takaful: Islamic insurance. islamic_deposit: Shariah-compliant deposit account. other: any asset not covered above.
category.description: The broad category of this asset. Determines which additional details may be relevant.

subcategory.description: More specific classification within the category. Free text to accommodate jurisdiction-specific asset types.

estimatedValue.description: Owner's estimate of the asset's current value, in minor currency units.

professionalValuation.description: Professional valuation of the asset, in minor currency units.

valuationDate.description: Date of the most recent valuation.

valuationConfidence.$comment: estimated: owner's own estimate. professional: assessed by a qualified valuer. probate: valuation agreed with tax authority for probate. unknown: basis of valuation is unknown.
valuationConfidence.description: Level of confidence in the stated valuation.

condition.$comment: excellent: like new or pristine. good: normal wear, fully functional. fair: showing age, may need minor repair. poor: significant wear, may need major repair. unknown: condition has not been assessed. not_applicable: condition is not relevant (e.g. bank accounts, pensions).
condition.description: Physical condition of the asset. Use 'not_applicable' for financial assets where condition is meaningless.

quantity.description: Number of items if this represents multiple identical or similar items.

location.description: Where this asset is physically stored or held — a bank, safe, property, or institution.

propertyId.description: Reference to a Property.id if this asset is located at a specific property in the estate.

identifiers.description: External identifiers — serial numbers, account numbers, policy references, registration numbers, etc.

images.description: Photographs, videos, and document scans of this asset. Use viewType to categorise each media item for efficient browsing by executors and dealers.

possessionStatus.$comment: possessed_at_death: asset was in the deceased's possession or control at death (muchzak in halacha). receivable: asset is owed to the estate but not yet received — e.g. insurance payouts, debts owed to the deceased (ra'ui in halacha). contingent: asset depends on a future event that may or may not occur. Under halacha, the bekhor's double portion applies only to 'possessed_at_death' (muchzak), not 'receivable' (ra'ui). Useful for probate valuations universally.
possessionStatus.description: Whether the asset was held at death, is receivable post-death, or is contingent on a future event.

mobilityType.$comment: immoveable: land or buildings (rare for assets — typically use Property). moveable: personal property, financial assets, vehicles. mixed: combination. Relevant for Shia inheritance and French lex rei sitae.
mobilityType.description: Whether this asset is classified as immoveable or moveable property. Affects which conflict-of-laws rules apply in cross-border estates.

acquisitionType.$comment: self_acquired: purchased or earned by the owner. ancestral_joint: undivided coparcenary property. ancestral_severed: partitioned former joint property. inherited: received through inheritance. gifted: received as a gift. stridhan: Hindu woman's absolute property. communal: communal or clan property. waqf_endowed: Islamic endowment.
acquisitionType.description: How this asset was acquired. Determines succession rules in Indian and other customary systems.

registrationStatus.$comment: formally_registered: documented with an official authority. informally_held: possessed but not registered. community_acknowledged: community recognises ownership. disputed: ownership is contested. undocumented: no documentation exists.
registrationStatus.description: Whether ownership of this asset is formally registered or documented.

ownershipEvidence.$comment: title_deed: formal ownership document. certificate_of_occupancy: Nigerian occupancy certificate. family_recognition: family acknowledges ownership. community_testimony: community can attest. receipts_only: purchase receipts only. none: no evidence available.
ownershipEvidence.description: The type of evidence available to prove ownership of this asset.

applicableSuccessionLaw.description: The succession law governing this specific asset, if different from the general estate law.

jurisdiction.description: Jurisdiction whose succession law governs this asset.

beneficiaryDesignation.$comment: For assets with named beneficiaries (retirement accounts, life insurance, POD bank accounts). These pass OUTSIDE the estate.
beneficiaryDesignation.description: Named beneficiary designation for this asset. Assets with beneficiary designations pass outside the probate estate — the will does not control their distribution.

primaryBeneficiaryPersonIds.description: Person IDs of the primary beneficiaries.

contingentBeneficiaryPersonIds.description: Person IDs of contingent beneficiaries (receive if primary beneficiaries predecease).

designationType.$comment: retirement_account: 401(k), IRA, SIPP, etc. life_insurance: life insurance policy. superannuation: Australian superannuation fund. pod_account: payable-on-death bank account. other: any other type of beneficiary designation.

linkedNonprobateTransferId.description: Reference to the NonprobateTransfer entity that tracks this designation.

digitalAccessConsent.$comment: RUFADAA (Revised Uniform Fiduciary Access to Digital Assets Act) — adopted by ~47 US states. Controls whether fiduciaries can access digital assets.
digitalAccessConsent.description: Digital access consent under RUFADAA or equivalent legislation. Governs whether executors and fiduciaries can access the deceased's digital accounts and assets.

consentGiven.description: Whether the account holder has given consent for fiduciary access.

scope.$comment: full_access: fiduciary may access all content and account data. limited_access: fiduciary may access only specified data (e.g. financial records but not personal messages). no_access: fiduciary may not access any content.
scope.description: The scope of access granted to fiduciaries.

designatedRecipientPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id designated to receive access to this digital asset.

onlineToolDirective.$comment: Whether the user set access directives via the platform's own tool (takes priority under RUFADAA).
onlineToolDirective.description: Whether the user set access directives through the platform's own tool (e.g. Google Inactive Account Manager, Facebook Legacy Contact). Platform directives take priority under RUFADAA.

passesOutsideEstate.$comment: True if this asset passes outside probate (beneficiary designation, POD, TOD). NOT distributed under the will.
passesOutsideEstate.description: Whether this asset passes outside the probate estate. Beneficiary designations, POD accounts, and jointly held assets typically bypass the will.

notes.description: Free-text notes about this asset. Use for provenance, sentimental value, special handling instructions, or anything not captured by structured fields.
examples: Belonged to great-grandmother — significant sentimental value to the family
Firearm requires Section 1 certificate — must be surrendered or transferred to a licence holder within 7 days of death

description.description: Structured description of the asset — what it is, its significance, and key details a dealer or executor would need. Distinct from 'notes' which is free-form.
examples: OO gauge Hornby R3456 Class 66 locomotive in EWS livery, DCC fitted, with original box and instructions
18ct yellow gold solitaire engagement ring, 1.2ct round brilliant diamond, GIA certified VS1/G

purchaseDate.description: Date the asset was acquired by the current owner. Used for capital gains tax calculations, insurance claims, and provenance. Maps to Schema.org purchaseDate.

originalPackaging.description: Whether original packaging, documentation, and accessories are present. Affects value by 20–40% across watches, model railways, jewellery, and many other domains.
originalPackaging.$comment: complete: original box, papers/certificates, all accessories present. partial: some original packaging elements present. box_only: original box but no papers. papers_only: certificates/papers but no box. none: no original packaging. unknown: packaging status not assessed.

custodian.description: Third party currently holding this asset — a bank (safe deposit), storage facility (bonded wine), repairer (watch at jeweller), gallery (art on loan), or institution. The executor needs to know who to contact.

name.description: Name of the person or organisation holding the asset.

relationship.description: The nature of the custodial relationship.

reference.description: Account or reference number with the custodian.

conditionSystem.description: The grading system used for the conditionGrade value. Allows domain-specific condition assessment alongside the generic condition field.
conditionSystem.$comment: goldmine: Goldmine Grading Standard for vinyl records (M/NM/VG+/VG/G+/G/F/P). sheldon: Sheldon scale for coins (1-70, where 70 is perfect). ags: American Gem Society for gemstones (0-10). bsc: British Standards for antiques. gia: Gemological Institute of America for diamonds.

conditionGrade.description: The specific grade within the conditionSystem. Meaningful only when conditionSystem is set.
